hooks.command: SCRIPTS_DIR="$HOME/.claude/scripts/elixir-phoenix-guide"; if [ -f "$SCRIPTS_DIR/detect_project.sh" ]; then bash "$SCRIPTS_DIR/detect_project.sh" 2>/dev/null; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Detect project stack (Phoenix version, LiveView, Ecto, Oban) and cache for context-aware hooks

hooks.command: INPUT=$(cat); if echo "$INPUT" | grep -qE 'mix\s+ecto\.reset'; then echo 'рџљ« Dangerous: mix ecto.reset destroys and recreates the database. Use mix ecto.rollback for safe rollbacks.' && exit 2; fi; if echo "$INPUT" | grep -qE 'git\s+push\s+.*(-f|--force)'; then echo 'рџљ« Dangerous: Force push overwrites remote history. Use --force-with-lease for safer force pushes.' && exit 2; fi; if echo "$INPUT" | grep -qE 'MIX_ENV=prod'; then echo 'рџљ« Dangerous: Running with MIX_ENV=prod locally risks unintended production effects.' && exit 2; fi
hooks.description: Block dangerous operations (ecto.reset, force push, MIX_ENV=prod)

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; CACHE="$(pwd)/.elixir-phoenix-guide-project.json"; if [ "$EXT" = "ex" ] || [ "$EXT" = "exs" ] || [ "$EXT" = "heex" ]; then SKIP_LV='false'; if [ -f "$CACHE" ]; then HAS_LV=$(grep -o '"has_liveview":\s*[a-z]*' "$CACHE" 2>/dev/null | grep -o '[a-z]*$'); if [ "$HAS_LV" = 'false' ]; then SKIP_LV='true'; fi; fi; if [ "$SKIP_LV" = 'true' ]; then echo 'рџ’Ў Reminder: API-only project detected (no LiveView). LiveView skills/hooks are inactive. Did you invoke the relevant elixir-phoenix-guide skill?'; else echo 'рџ’Ў Reminder: Did you invoke the relevant elixir-phoenix-guide skill before writing this file? If not, invoke it now and verify your code follows the rules.'; fi; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Remind about skill invocation for Elixir files (context-aware: notes API-only projects)

hooks.command: CACHE="$(pwd)/.elixir-phoenix-guide-project.json"; if [ -f "$CACHE" ]; then HAS_LV=$(grep -o '"has_liveview":\s*[a-z]*' "$CACHE" 2>/dev/null | grep -o '[a-z]*$'); if [ "$HAS_LV" = 'false' ]; then exit 0; fi; fi; FILTERED=$(grep -v '^\s*#' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$FILTERED" | grep -qE 'def\s+(mount|handle_event|handle_info|handle_call|handle_cast|render|init|terminate)\(' && ! echo "$FILTERED" | grep -B1 'def\s\+(mount|handle_event|handle_info|handle_call|handle_cast|render|init|terminate)\(' | grep -q '@impl'; then echo 'вљ пёЏ  Missing @impl true before callback function.' && echo '' && echo '   рџ’Ў Fix: Add @impl true on the line before each callback:' && echo '' && echo '     @impl true' && echo '     def mount(params, session, socket) do' && echo '       ...' && echo '     end' && echo '' && echo '   Applies to: mount, handle_event, handle_info, handle_call, handle_cast, render, init, terminate' && exit 2; fi
hooks.description: Block callbacks without @impl true вЂ” with fix suggestion (context-aware: skips in API-only projects)

hooks.command: if grep -qE '(upload_path|file_path|uploads_dir)\s*=\s*["'\''](/|priv/)' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo 'рџљ« Hardcoded file path detected.' && echo '' && echo '   рџ’Ў Fix: Move to Application config:' && echo '' && echo '     # config/config.exs' && echo '     config :my_app, :upload_path, "priv/static/uploads"' && echo '' && echo '     # In your module' && echo '     upload_path = Application.get_env(:my_app, :upload_path)' && exit 2; fi
hooks.description: Block hardcoded file paths вЂ” with fix suggestion

hooks.command: if grep -qE '(max_file_size|file_size_limit|max_upload|max_size)\s*=\s*[0-9]{7,}' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo 'рџљ« Hardcoded file size limit detected (7+ digits).' && echo '' && echo '   рџ’Ў Fix: Move to Application config:' && echo '' && echo '     # config/config.exs' && echo '     config :my_app, :max_upload_size, 10_000_000' && echo '' && echo '     # In your module' && echo '     max_size = Application.get_env(:my_app, :max_upload_size)' && exit 2; fi
hooks.description: Block hardcoded file size limits вЂ” with fix suggestion

hooks.command: COLLAPSED=$(tr '\n' ' ' < "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$COLLAPSED" | grep -qE 'if\s+[^d]+\s+do\s+[^e]*if\s+[^d]+\s+do'; then echo 'вљ пёЏ  Nested if/else detected.' && echo '' && echo '   рџ’Ў Fix: Replace with case or multi-clause function:' && echo '' && echo '     # Instead of nested if/else:' && echo '     case {condition_a, condition_b} do' && echo '       {true, true} -> handle_both()' && echo '       {true, false} -> handle_a_only()' && echo '       _ -> handle_default()' && echo '     end' && echo '' && echo '     # Or use multi-clause functions:' && echo '     defp handle(true, true), do: ...' && echo '     defp handle(true, false), do: ...' && echo '     defp handle(_, _), do: ...' && exit 1; fi
hooks.description: Warn on nested if/else вЂ” with case/multi-clause fix suggestion

hooks.command: COLLAPSED=$(tr '\n' ' ' < "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$COLLAPSED" | grep -qE '\|>\s*Enum\.(map|filter)\([^)]+\)\s*\|>\s*Enum\.(map|filter)\('; then echo 'вљ пёЏ  Multiple Enum.map/filter chain detected.' && echo '' && echo '   рџ’Ў Fix: Use a for comprehension or combine into one pass:' && echo '' && echo '     # Instead of: list |> Enum.filter(&active?/1) |> Enum.map(&transform/1)' && echo '     for item <- list, active?(item), do: transform(item)' && echo '' && echo '     # Or use Enum.reduce for complex transformations:' && echo '     Enum.reduce(list, [], fn item, acc ->' && echo '       if active?(item), do: [transform(item) | acc], else: acc' && echo '     end) |> Enum.reverse()' && exit 1; fi
hooks.description: Warn on inefficient Enum chains вЂ” with for comprehension fix suggestion

hooks.command: if grep -qE 'Enum\.(map|reduce|each).*<>' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo 'вљ пёЏ  String concatenation with <> in Enum operations.' && echo '' && echo '   рџ’Ў Fix: Use IO lists or Enum.join:' && echo '' && echo '     # Instead of: Enum.map(items, &(prefix <> &1))' && echo '     # Use IO lists (zero-copy, best performance):' && echo '     Enum.map(items, &[prefix, &1])' && echo '' && echo '     # Or Enum.join for simple cases:' && echo '     Enum.join(items, ", ")' && exit 1; fi
hooks.description: Warn on string concatenation in loops вЂ” with IO list/Enum.join fix suggestion

hooks.command: if grep -qE 'def static_paths' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then PATHS=$(grep -A10 'def static_paths' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -oE '"[^"]+"' | tr -d '"' | tr '\n' ' '); REFS=$(grep -hoE '/[a-z_]+/' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | sort -u | tr -d '/' | tr '\n' ' '); for ref in $REFS; do echo " $PATHS " | grep -qw "$ref" || echo "вљ пёЏ  Path reference '/$ref/' found but '$ref' not in static_paths(). Add to lib/my_app_web.ex: def static_paths, do: ~w(assets $ref favicon.ico)" && exit 2; done; fi
hooks.description: Block file path references not in static_paths

hooks.command: CACHE="$(pwd)/.elixir-phoenix-guide-project.json"; HAS_SCOPE='false'; if [ -f "$CACHE" ]; then HAS_SCOPE=$(grep -o '"phoenix_has_scope":\s*[a-z]*' "$CACHE" 2>/dev/null | grep -o '[a-z]*$'); fi; if grep -qE '<\.(flash_group|flash)' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo 'рџљ« .flash_group component is deprecated in Phoenix 1.8+. Flash handling is now automatic in layouts. Remove <.flash_group> calls.' && exit 2; fi; if grep -qE 'form_for\(' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo 'рџљ« form_for is deprecated. Use <.form for={to_form(@changeset)}> instead.' && exit 2; fi; if grep -qE 'live_redirect|live_patch' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo 'рџљ« live_redirect/live_patch are deprecated. Use <.link navigate={path}> or push_navigate/push_patch instead.' && exit 2; fi; if [ "$HAS_SCOPE" = 'true' ] && grep -qE '@current_user|current_user' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null && ! grep -qE '@current_scope|current_scope' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo 'вљ пёЏ  Phoenix 1.8+ detected: Use @current_scope instead of @current_user. Access user via @current_scope.user' && exit 1; fi
hooks.description: Block deprecated Phoenix components вЂ” context-aware: warns on @current_user in Phoenix 1.8+ projects

hooks.command: CACHE="$(pwd)/.elixir-phoenix-guide-project.json"; HAS_LV='true'; if [ -f "$CACHE" ]; then HAS_LV=$(grep -o '"has_liveview":\s*[a-z]*' "$CACHE" 2>/dev/null | grep -o '[a-z]*$'); fi; if [ "$HAS_LV" = 'false' ]; then exit 0; fi; if grep -qE 'auto_upload:\s*true' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo 'вљ пёЏ  auto_upload: true detected. This requires special handling with handle_progress/3. Most apps should use manual upload (remove auto_upload: true and add a submit button). See phoenix-uploads skill for details.' && exit 1; fi
hooks.description: Warn on auto_upload usage (context-aware: skips in API-only projects)

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; if [ "$EXT" = "ex" ] || [ "$EXT" = "exs" ]; then if ! echo "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '_test\.exs$|/test/'; then if grep -qE 'IO\.inspect\(|dbg\(|IO\.puts\(' "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; then echo 'вљ пёЏ  Debug statement detected (IO.inspect, dbg, or IO.puts). Remove before committing вЂ” these should not reach production.' && exit 1; fi; fi; fi
hooks.description: Warn on debug statements outside test files (IO.inspect, dbg, IO.puts)

hooks.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE 'mix\.exs$'; then echo 'рџ’Ў Dependencies file (mix.exs) modified. Consider running:'; echo '   mix deps.audit  - check for known vulnerabilities'; echo '   mix hex.audit   - verify package checksums'; echo '   mix sobelow     - static security analysis'; exit 0; fi
hooks.description: Remind about security auditing when mix.exs is modified
Check migrations for missing FK indexes, cascade strategies, and unsafe operations

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; SCRIPTS_DIR="$HOME/.claude/scripts/elixir-phoenix-guide"; if [ "$EXT" = "ex" ] || [ "$EXT" = "exs" ]; then if command -v elixir >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ -f "$SCRIPTS_DIR/code_quality.exs" ]; then elixir "$SCRIPTS_DIR/code_quality.exs" all "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; exit $?; fi; elif [ "$EXT" = "heex" ]; then if [ -f "$SCRIPTS_DIR/detect_template_duplication.sh" ]; then bash "$SCRIPTS_DIR/detect_template_duplication.sh" "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null; exit $?; fi; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Code quality analysis вЂ” duplication, complexity, unused functions (Elixir files) and template duplication (HEEx files)

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; if [ "$EXT" != "ex" ] && [ "$EXT" != "exs" ]; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '_test\.exs$|/test/'; then exit 0; fi; CONTENT=$(cat "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE '\.(posts|comments|users|items|entries|tasks|categories|tags|orders|products|messages|notifications|accounts|roles|permissions|memberships|addresses|invoices|images|attachments|events|sessions|tokens)\b' && ! echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE 'preload|Repo\.preload|from.*preload|join.*assoc'; then echo 'вљ пёЏ  Possible missing preload: Association accessor found without a visible preload.' && echo '' && echo '   рџ’Ў Fix: Preload associations in your query:' && echo '' && echo '     # In the context function:' && echo '     Repo.all(from p in Post, preload: [:comments, :user])' && echo '' && echo '     # Or after fetch:' && echo '     post |> Repo.preload(:comments)' && echo '' && echo '   Without preload, accessing associations raises Ecto.Association.NotLoaded.' && exit 1; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Warn on possible missing preload вЂ” association accessed without visible preload

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; if [ "$EXT" != "ex" ] && [ "$EXT" != "exs" ]; then exit 0; fi; CONTENT=$(cat "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE 'with\s' && ! echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE 'with.*do.*else|else\s*do'; then COLLAPSED=$(echo "$CONTENT" | tr '\n' ' '); if echo "$COLLAPSED" | grep -qE 'with\s+[^}]+<-[^}]+do\s+[^}]+end' && ! echo "$COLLAPSED" | grep -qE 'with\s+[^}]+<-[^}]+do\s+[^}]+else[^}]+end'; then echo 'вљ пёЏ  with statement without else clause.' && echo '' && echo '   рџ’Ў Fix: Add an else clause to handle errors:' && echo '' && echo '     with {:ok, user} <- fetch_user(id),' && echo '          {:ok, post} <- create_post(user, attrs) do' && echo '       {:ok, post}' && echo '     else' && echo '       {:error, :not_found} -> {:error, :user_not_found}' && echo '       {:error, changeset} -> {:error, changeset}' && echo '     end' && echo '' && echo '   Without else, non-matching values are returned as-is, which can leak internal errors.' && exit 1; fi; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Warn on with statement missing else clause вЂ” suggests error handling pattern

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; if [ "$EXT" != "ex" ] && [ "$EXT" != "exs" ]; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '_test\.exs$|/test/'; then exit 0; fi; CONTENT=$(cat "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE 'Ecto\.Adapters\.SQL\.(query|query!)|SQL\.query'; then if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE '".*#\{.*\}.*"|".*\$\{.*\}.*"' || echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE 'query.*<>|<>.*query'; then echo 'рџљ« Raw SQL with string interpolation detected вЂ” SQL injection risk!' && echo '' && echo '   рџ’Ў Fix: Use parameterized queries:' && echo '' && echo '     # Instead of: Ecto.Adapters.SQL.query(Repo, "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = #{id}")' && echo '     Ecto.Adapters.SQL.query(Repo, "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1", [id])' && echo '' && echo '   Always pass user input as parameters, never interpolate into SQL strings.' && exit 2; fi; echo 'вљ пёЏ  Raw SQL query detected (Ecto.Adapters.SQL.query).' && echo '' && echo '   рџ’Ў Prefer Ecto query syntax when possible:' && echo '' && echo '     from(u in User, where: u.id == ^id) |> Repo.one()' && echo '' && echo '   If raw SQL is necessary, always use parameterized queries ($1, $2, ...) and never interpolate user input.' && exit 1; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Warn on raw SQL queries вЂ” blocks string interpolation (SQL injection), suggests parameterized queries

hooks.command: EXT="${CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH##*.}"; if [ "$EXT" != "ex" ]; then exit 0; fi; CACHE="$(pwd)/.elixir-phoenix-guide-project.json"; HAS_LV='true'; if [ -f "$CACHE" ]; then HAS_LV=$(grep -o '"has_liveview":\s*[a-z]*' "$CACHE" 2>/dev/null | grep -o '[a-z]*$'); fi; if [ "$HAS_LV" = 'false' ]; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" | grep -qE '_live\.ex$|_live/|live/'; then CONTENT=$(cat "$CLAUDE_HOOK_FILE_PATH" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$CONTENT" | grep -qE 'Repo\.(all|one|get|get!|get_by|insert|update|delete|aggregate|exists\?|preload)'; then echo 'вљ пёЏ  Context boundary violation: Repo called directly in a LiveView module.' && echo '' && echo '   рџ’Ў Fix: Move database access to a context module:' && echo '' && echo '     # Instead of (in LiveView):' && echo '     #   posts = Repo.all(Post)' && echo '' && echo '     # Create a context function:' && echo '     #   defmodule MyApp.Blog do' && echo '     #     def list_posts, do: Repo.all(Post)' && echo '     #   end' && echo '' && echo '     # Call from LiveView:' && echo '     #   posts = Blog.list_posts()' && echo '' && echo '   LiveViews should never access Repo directly вЂ” use context functions to maintain boundaries.' && exit 1; fi; fi; exit 0
hooks.description: Warn on Repo calls in LiveView modules вЂ” context boundary violation (context-aware: skips in API-only projects)

hooks.command:
echo '## Elixir Phoenix Guide вЂ” Rules for Subagents

You are working in an Elixir/Phoenix project. Follow these rules strictly.

### Elixir Rules
1. Use pattern matching over if/else for control flow
2. Add @impl true before every callback function
3. Return {:ok, result} | {:error, reason} tuples for fallible operations
4. Use with for 2+ sequential fallible operations
5. Use the pipe operator for 2+ chained transformations
6. Never nest if/else вЂ” use case, cond, or multi-clause functions
7. Let it crash вЂ” no defensive code for impossible states

### LiveView Rules
1. Always add @impl true before every callback
2. Initialize ALL assigns in mount/3
3. Check connected?(socket) before PubSub subscriptions or side effects
4. Return proper tuples вЂ” {:ok, socket} from mount, {:noreply, socket} from handle_event
5. Never use auto_upload: true with form submission
6. Check core_components.ex before creating custom components
7. Never query the database directly from LiveViews вЂ” use context functions

### Ecto Rules
1. Always use changesets for inserts/updates вЂ” never raw maps
2. Preload associations before accessing them вЂ” avoid N+1
3. Use transactions for multi-step operations
4. Add database constraints AND changeset validations
5. Use contexts for database access вЂ” never call Repo from web layer
6. Add indexes on foreign keys and frequently queried fields

### OTP Rules
1. Always use @impl true before GenServer callbacks
2. Keep init/1 fast вЂ” use handle_continue for expensive setup
3. Use call for request/response, cast for fire-and-forget
4. Define a public API wrapping GenServer calls
5. Use Task.async/Task.await with bounded timeouts
6. Name processes via Registry, not atoms
7. Supervisors own process lifecycle вЂ” no unsupervised long-running processes

### Oban Rules
1. Always use Oban.Worker with explicit queue and max_attempts
2. Return {:ok, result}, {:error, reason}, or {:cancel, reason}
3. Make workers idempotent
4. Use unique option to prevent duplicate jobs
5. Test with Oban.Testing вЂ” use assert_enqueued and perform_job
6. Never put large data in job args вЂ” store IDs, fetch in worker

### Testing Rules
1. Use DataCase for DB tests, ConnCase for LiveView/controller tests
2. Test both happy path AND error cases
3. Use async: true only when safe
4. Define test data in fixtures вЂ” never inline across tests
5. Use has_element?/2 for LiveView assertions
6. Always test the unauthorized case

### Upload Rules
1. Use manual uploads, not auto_upload: true
2. Add upload directory to static_paths()
3. Generate unique filenames вЂ” prevent collisions and path traversal
4. Validate file types server-side

### Code Quality Rules
1. Extract duplicated functions (>70% similar) into shared modules
2. Keep function ABC complexity below 30
3. Remove unused private functions
4. Extract duplicated template markup into function components

### LiveView Auth Rules
1. Use on_mount callbacks for LiveView auth вЂ” never check in mount/3 directly
2. Use mount_current_scope/2 to extract scope from session
3. Handle both :cont and :halt returns from on_mount
4. Resolve Phoenix.Controller/Phoenix.LiveView import conflicts with except:
5. Use assigns[:current_scope] (bracket access) in templates вЂ” dot access crashes on nil
6. Test auth redirects with {:error, {:redirect, %{to: path}}}
7. Define on_mount hooks once, reference via live_session in router

### Changeset Rules
1. Create separate named changesets per operation (registration, email, password)
2. Never require foreign key fields in cast_assoc child changesets
3. Compose changesets with pipes вЂ” each validation is a separate function
4. Pair unsafe_validate_unique with unique_constraint вЂ” never one without the other
5. Use update_change/3 for field transforms вЂ” never transform in controllers
6. Accept opts \\\\ [] for conditional validation

### Auth Customization Rules
1. Never modify generated auth migrations вЂ” create separate migrations
2. Update registration_changeset for new fields
3. Update test fixtures when adding required fields
4. Confirm users in fixtures вЂ” confirmed_at: DateTime.utc_now(:second)
5. Update both form AND save handler for new registration fields

### PubSub Rules
1. Guard subscriptions with if connected?(socket)
2. Broadcast from contexts, not LiveViews
3. Use consistent topic naming вЂ” resource:id for specific, resource:action for collection
4. Handle PubSub messages in handle_info/2, never handle_event/3
5. Update assigns immutably with update/3

### Authorization Rules
1. Always authorize on the server in event handlers вЂ” UI checks are not security
2. Verify ownership: current_scope.user.id == resource.user_id
3. Use policy modules for complex authorization
4. Test both authorized and unauthorized paths
5. Scope queries to current user вЂ” where(user_id: ^user_id)

### Nested Association Rules
1. Use cast_assoc/3 for has_many/has_one вЂ” never manually insert children
2. Use Ecto.Multi for operations spanning unrelated tables
3. Set on_delete explicitly вЂ” :delete_all for owned children, :nothing for references
4. Always create indexes on foreign key columns
5. Use on_replace: :delete for list management in cast_assoc
6. Preload associations before updating вЂ” cast_assoc needs loaded data'
hooks.description: Inject condensed plugin rules into every subagent
